chapter_title.ar: بَابُ دَعَوَاتٍ مُوجَزَاتٍ لِجَمِيعِ الْحَوَائِجِ لِلدُّنْيَا وَ الْآخِرَةِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 60 – Brief supplications for the entirety of the needs of the world and the Hereafter

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/472)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: موثق - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/472)

parts.text: أَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) أَنَّ رَجُلًا أَتَى أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام )

text: يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ كَانَ لِي مَالٌ وَرِثْتُهُ وَ لَمْ أُنْفِقْ مِنْهُ دِرْهَماً فِي طَاعَةِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ ثُمَّ اكْتَسَبْتُ مِنْهُ مَالًا فَلَمْ أُنْفِقْ مِنْهُ دِرْهَماً فِي طَاعَةِ اللَّهِ فَعَلِّمْنِي دُعَاءً يُخْلِفُ عَلَيَّ مَا مَضَى وَ يَغْفِرُ لِي مَا عَمِلْتُ أَوْ عَمَلًا أَعْمَلُهُ

قَالَ قُلْ قَالَ وَ أَيَّ شَيْ‏ءٍ أَقُولُ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ قَالَ قُلْ كَمَا أَقُولُ يَا نُورِي فِي كُلِّ ظُلْمَةٍ وَ يَا أُنْسِي فِي كُلِّ وَحْشَةٍ وَ يَا رَجَائِي فِي كُلِّ كُرْبَةٍ وَ يَا ثِقَتِي فِي كُلِّ شِدَّةٍ وَ يَا دَلِيلِي فِي الضَّلَالَةِ أَنْتَ دَلِيلِي إِذَا انْقَطَعَتْ دَلَالَةُ الْأَدِلَّاءِ فَإِنَّ دَلَالَتَكَ لَا تَنْقَطِعُ وَ لَا يَضِلُّ مَنْ هَدَيْتَ

أَنْعَمْتَ عَلَيَّ فَأَسْبَغْتَ وَ رَزَقْتَنِي فَوَفَّرْتَ وَ غَذَّيْتَنِي فَأَحْسَنْتَ غِذَائِي وَ أَعْطَيْتَنِي فَأَجْزَلْتَ بِلَا اسْتِحْقَاقٍ لِذَلِكَ بِفِعْلٍ مِنِّي وَ لَكِنِ ابْتِدَاءً مِنْكَ لِكَرَمِكَ وَ جُودِكَ

فَتَقَوَّيْتُ بِكَرَمِكَ عَلَى مَعَاصِيكَ وَ تَقَوَّيْتُ بِرِزْقِكَ عَلَى سَخَطِكَ وَ أَفْنَيْتُ عُمُرِي فِيمَا لَا تُحِبُّ فَلَمْ يَمْنَعْكَ جُرْأَتِي عَلَيْكَ وَ رُكُوبِي لِمَا نَهَيْتَنِي عَنْهُ وَ دُخُولِي فِيمَا حَرَّمْتَ عَلَيَّ أَنْ عُدْتَ عَلَيَّ بِفَضْلِكَ وَ لَمْ يَمْنَعْنِي حِلْمُكَ عَنِّي وَ عَوْدُكَ عَلَيَّ بِفَضْلِكَ أَنْ عُدْتُ فِي مَعَاصِيكَ

فَأَنْتَ الْعَوَّادُ بِالْفَضْلِ وَ أَنَا الْعَوَّادُ بِالْمَعَاصِي فَيَا أَكْرَمَ مَنْ أُقِرَّ لَهُ بِذَنْبٍ وَ أَعَزَّ مَنْ خُضِعَ لَهُ بِذُلٍّ لِكَرَمِكَ أَقْرَرْتُ بِذَنْبِي وَ لِعِزِّكَ خَضَعْتُ بِذُلِّي فَمَا أَنْتَ صَانِعٌ بِي فِي كَرَمِكَ وَ إِقْرَارِي بِذَنْبِي وَ عِزِّكَ وَ خُضُوعِي بِذُلِّي افْعَلْ بِي مَا أَنْتَ أَهْلُهُ وَ لَا تَفْعَلْ بِي مَا أَنَا أَهْلُهُ

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Hamza, from one of his companions,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> that a man came over to Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup> and he said, ‘O Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>! There used to be wealth for me. I had inherited it and did not spend a Dirham from it in the obedience of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic. Then I earned wealth from it, but I did not spend Dirham from it in the obedience of Allah<sup>azwj</sup>. So teach me a supplication replacing upon me what is past and He<sup>azwj</sup> would Forgive me what I have done or work that I am doing’.

He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Say!’ He said, ‘And which thing should I be saying, O Amir Al-Momineen<sup>asws</sup>?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Say just as I<sup>asws</sup> am saying: ‘O my Light in every darkness, and O my Comfort in every loneliness, and O my Hope in every distress, and of My Reliance in every difficulty, and O my Direction during the straying! You<sup>azwj</sup> are my Guidance when the guidance of the guides is cut off, for Your<sup>azwj</sup> Guidance doe not  get cut off, nor does he go astray, the one whom You<sup>azwj</sup> Guide.

You<sup>azwj</sup> Bestowed upon me, so You<sup>azwj</sup> were Liberal, and You<sup>azwj</sup> Graced me sustenance, so You<sup>azwj</sup> were Ample in provision, and You<sup>azwj</sup> Fed me with excellent of my food, and You Gave to me, so You<sup>azwj</sup> (Chose) the best without me being deserving of that by any deed from me, but Initiated from You<sup>azwj</sup> due to Your<sup>azwj</sup> Benevolence and Your<sup>azwj</sup> generousity.

Thus, I was strengthened upon my disobedience due to Your<sup>azwj</sup> Benevolence, and was strengthened upon Your<sup>azwj</sup> Wrath by Your<sup>azwj</sup> sustenance, and I finished my life regarding what You<sup>azwj</sup> do not Love, but You<sup>azwj</sup> did not Deprive me despite my audacity to You<sup>azwj</sup> and my indulging in what You<sup>azwj</sup> had Forbidden from and my entering into what You<sup>azwj</sup> had Prohibited upon me, You<sup>azwj</sup> (still) Returned upon me with Your<sup>azwj</sup> Grace, and did not Deprive me of Your<sup>azwj</sup> Forbearance from me and Your<sup>azwj</sup> Cordiality upon me with Your<sup>azwj</sup> Grace even when I returned to be in Your<sup>azwj</sup> disobedience.

So You<sup>azwj</sup> are the Liberal with the Grace and I am the liberal in my disobedience. So O Benevolent to the one who acknowledges with Him<sup>azwj</sup>, and Honourable to the one who succumbs to Him<sup>azwj</sup> with humiliation to Your<sup>azwj</sup> Benevolence, I hereby acknowledge with my sins and I humble myself to Your<sup>azwj</sup> Might with my humbleness. So whatever You<sup>azwj</sup> Do with me in Your<sup>azwj</sup> Benevolence and my acknowledgement with my sins, and Your<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and my subservience with my humiliation, Treat me with what You<sup>azwj</sup> are Rightful of and do not Treat with me what I am deserving of’’.
en.sarwar: 35. Ali ibn abu Hamza has narrated from certain individuals of his people from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Once a man came to Amir al-Mu’minin, Ali ibn abu Talib (a.s.) and said, ‘O Amir al- Mu’minin, I inherited a property but did not give even one dirham in obedience to Allah, the Most Majestic, the Most Holy. I thereafter made an earning and did not spend anything in obedience to Allah. Instruct me with a supplication that can replace for me things of the past, and what I have done be forgiven as well as what I may do.’ The narrator has said that the Imam told him, ‘Say.’ The man asked, ‘What should I say, O Amir al-Mu’minin?’ “The Imam said, ‘Say as I say, “O my light in all darkness, O my comfort in all dreadful conditions, O my hope in all painful matters, O my trust in every hardship, O my guide in all straying conditions. You are my guide, when all guidance of guides is cut off but Your guidance remains. Whoever You guide does not stray off the right path. You granted me favors and made it delightful. You provided me sustenance in abundance, You fed me and made my food best, Y ou bestowed upon me and made it the best gift without my deserving it by means of my deeds but initiated from You due to Your magnanimity and generosity. I found strength from Your magnanimity to disobey You, I gained power from Your sustenance but I disappointed You, 1 ruined my life in what You disliked, my daring against You, my committing what You had forbidden, my entering in things You prohibited, did not prevent You from turning to me with Your generosity, Your forbearance did not hold You back from doing favors to me even if I returned to sin and disobedience to You, You always turn back with generosity and bounty but 1 always sin. How magnanimous You are to those who confess before You to their sins! “How majestic You are to those who humble themselves before You! Before Your magnanimity I confess my sins and for Your majesty I humble myself before You. It now is all up to You what You will do to me in Your magnanimity and with my confessing my sins before You for Your majesty and my begging respectfully, I plead before You to deal with me the way it is worthy of You not according to what I deserve.’”
